a_image.alt: Dutch Design style mood board — Regional Style · Netherlands

sections.a_description: Bold primary colors, sharp geometric forms, confident typography, and the playful-yet-structured spirit of a nation that gave the world De Stijl and Mondrian — Dutch Design translates centuries of graphic excellence into digital form. The bright blues and oranges, razor-sharp edges, and generous negative space create interfaces that feel simultaneously experimental and meticulously organized.

This bold geometric style influences design studios, cultural institutions, architecture firms, and creative agencies worldwide. You'll see its DNA in museum websites, design festival programs, Dutch government portals, and avant-garde editorial layouts — anywhere that values the intersection of functional clarity and creative boldness that defines the Netherlands' design heritage.

a_image_cover.alt: Dutch Design style use case illustration
Dutch Design style use case illustration
Dutch Design style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The bold, experimental, geometry-driven aesthetic of Dutch graphic design — playful yet structured, combining De Stijl's primary-color boldness with contemporary typographic experimentation. Background: clean white (#ffffff). The .inner panel has a white background with a thick left border composed of stacked color blocks — a 4px solid accent in bright blue (#0057ff) — nodding to Mondrian's geometric compositions. Colors: primary bright blue (#0057ff) for accents and interactive elements. Warm orange (#ff6d00) as a secondary highlight. Pure black (#000000) for headings, dark gray (#444444) for body text. The aesthetic is confident, structured yet playful — bold geometric forms, strong color blocking, and generous use of negative space. Set in Work Sans — a geometric sans-serif that captures the clean, functional character of Dutch typography. Heading at weight 800, 28px, line-height 1.2, letter-spacing -0.5px, color #000000. Eyebrow at weight 600, 11px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #0057ff. Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7, color #444444. Buttons: primary — bright blue (#0057ff), white text, 0 border-radius (sharp geometric edges). Secondary: white background, blue border. Alternatives: Overpass (geometric Dutch-inspired), Jost (Bauhaus-influenced geometric).
